title: Agricultural Production, Irrigation, Climate Change, and Water Scarcity in India

summary.about: How climate change and limited water supplies could reshape Indian agriculture, irrigation, land use and welfare.
summary.audience: Agricultural, water, climate and food-security policymakers.
summary.method: Combines an advanced CGE model with biophysical crop-yield projections and water-scarcity constraints.
summary.insights: Water scarcity reverses possible climate-related gains, reduces irrigation and could create welfare losses of about $3.2 billion in 2030.
